title: After students, teachers also protest on Thursday against cuts to French-speaking education

news.title: After students, teachers also protest on Thursday against cuts to French-speaking education

world.title: After students, teachers also protest on Thursday against cuts to French-speaking education

environment.compositionIntent: full-surround vertical stripe pressure with a central white civic aperture, cropped diagonals, and layered Riley-style grid interruptions for Against Cuts

contrast.notes: warm paper light keeps matte hard-edge planes flat while a restrained teal rim separates central rings and diagonal support marks

guidance: Use for painted, paper, canvas, ink wash, graphite, stain, and transfer surfaces.
Represent texture through declarative surface controls and WebGPU-safe texture descriptors.
Use for all-over color fields, hard-edged planes, atmospheric bands, and flat graphic fields.
Prefer geometry layers or declarative texture descriptors over GLSL gradients.
Use for soft atmosphere, dust, floating pigment, and particulate halos.
Keep the particle logic deterministic and inspectable in data.
Use for WebGPU-safe color grading and output transforms.
Do not use legacy GLSL post passes for WebGPU project scenes.

params.compositionUse: dominant full-surround stripe shell, cropped diagonals, ring aperture, and far-line pressure field

workshopAcceptance: Wrote the final ES module to drafts/world-environment.module.mjs.
Static check succeeded with validation.pass true and no errors; package compatibility reported webgpuCompatible true with MeshBasicMaterial and LineBasicMaterial evidence and no forbidden surfaces.

Playwright preview attempt 1 succeeded for desktop. Screenshot shows a dominant full-surround Bridget Riley-derived stripe environment with central white aperture, radial rings, cropped diagonals, teal/warm-earth counterpoints, and dense black/red grid pressure.
Render report healthStatus ok, captureStatus ok, nonBlankRatio 0.893482, centerNonBlankRatio 0.929572, dynamicRange 255, deadSpaceRatio 0.034622.

selfReview.summary: Accepted: real dominant environment module with preview-backed evidence.
strengths: Artist/news signal is visible through vertical stripe systems, radial aperture, hard-edge diagonals, matte paper palette, teal counterpoints, and black/red pressure lines.
Uses WebGPU-safe standard Three.js material surfaces; no ShaderMaterial, RawShaderMaterial, large DataTexture baking, imports, fetch, timers, or ambient randomness.
concerns: Preview runtime reported webgl-legacy renderer, but generated-module compatibility evidence reports webgpuCompatible true from safe material surfaces.

compositionAdaptations: Kept as the dominant full-surround stripe shell with warm paper environment settings and canonical camera framing.

params.compositionUse: dominant near-central target, diagonal pressure planes, cut-lattice protest signal, and final white aperture interruption

workshopAcceptance: Inspected local concept reference image directly: broad cue used as advisory only for warm paper, vertical stripe fields, cropped diagonals, concentric target, dark line pressure, and teal counterpoint.
Static validation succeeded for drafts/non-literal-grid-pressure.module.mjs with validation.pass true and no errors.
Attempt 3 Playwright desktop preview captured successfully at previews/attempt-003/003-non-literal-grid-pressure-draft-desktop.jpg.
Attempt 3 render report status ok: readiness ready, health ok, capture ok, nonBlankRatio 0.986298, dynamicRange 255, centerCoverageRatio 0.724931.
Attempt 3 package report marked the module webgpu-compatible through MeshBasicMaterial and LineBasicMaterial surfaces with forbiddenSurfaces [].

selfReview.summary: Accepted: the module is a real WebGPU-safe Three.js part, readable in isolation, with Bridget Riley-inspired stripe/ring/diagonal systems carrying the selected education-cuts protest pressure.
strengths: Strong central subject with white aperture and radial-symbol target.
Clear vertical grid pressure, cropped diagonals, matte warm-earth palette, dark hatching, and teal counterpoints.
No shader surfaces, imports, network, ambient randomness, or CPU-baked textures.
concerns: Part preview runtime reported webgl-legacy backend for capture, but package compatibility evidence marks the module webgpu-compatible with only standard safe material surfaces.

compositionAdaptations: Assigned primary foreground params and higher scale metadata to preserve the intended central pressure/aperture signal.

params.compositionUse: right-side supporting stripe rectangle and mark-density brace, kept small enough to leave the primary central aperture and pressure rings readable

workshopAcceptance: Inspected the local loose concept reference image directly for broad palette, value balance, density, and central target/stripe mood only.
Static draft validation succeeded with no bundling errors or forbidden-surface validation errors.

Final Playwright preview attempt 3 rendered a nonblank, camera-safe Bridget Riley mark field: enlarged matte paper backing, vertical moire stripe pressure, hard-edge cut lattice, radial barcode ticks, warm-earth/pale pink/teal palette, and a central white aperture interruption.
Final render report healthStatus was ok with captureStatus ok, nonBlankRatio 0.999824, centerCoverageRatio 0.891221, dynamicRange 254.667, and no browser failure reported.

selfReview.summary: Accepted: the final draft is a real WebGPU-safe Three.js artist mark field, not a placeholder or fallback, and preview evidence supports handoff.
strengths: Uses only standard Three.js geometry and materials: MeshBasicMaterial, LineBasicMaterial, RingGeometry, CircleGeometry, PlaneGeometry, BufferGeometry, and InstancedMesh.
Preserves Bridget Riley style facts through crisp vertical stripe systems, radial-symbol pressure, hard-edge cuts, optical hatching, matte screenprint-like surfaces, and controlled palette contrast.
Carries the news cue non-literally as measured organized pressure around a central civic aperture.
concerns: Preview renderer reported webgl-legacy backend for the local capture route, but module validation/package evidence stayed on WebGPU-safe surfaces and no forbidden shader or CPU-baked texture paths were used.
